title: Canto 10 Chapter 76
summary: Complete English Bhagavatam chapter generated from the public gita/Datasets repository. Each route exposes one shloka at a time.
sourceNote: This chapter includes devanagari text, roman transliteration, English translation, and purport when available in the upstream corpus.

verses.text: श्रीशुक उवाचअथान्यदपि कृष्णस्य श‍ृणु कर्माद्भ‍ुतं नृप ।क्रीडानरशरीरस्य यथा सौभपतिर्हत: ॥ १ ॥
verses.transliteration: śrī-śuka uvācaathānyad api kṛṣṇasyaśṛṇu karmādbhutaṁ nṛpakrīḍā-nara-śarīrasyayathā saubha-patir hataḥ
verses.meaning: Śukadeva Gosvāmī said: Now please hear, O King, another wondrous deed performed by Lord Kṛṣṇa, who appeared in His humanlike body to enjoy transcendental pastimes. Hear how He killed the master of Saubha.
verses.wordMeanings: śrī; śukaḥ; uvāca; atha; anyat; api; kṛṣṇasya; śṛṇu; karma; adbhutam; nṛpa; krīḍā; nara; śarīrasya; yathā; saubha; patiḥ; hataḥ

verses.text: शिशुपालसख: शाल्वो रुक्‍मिण्युद्वाह आगत: ।यदुभिर्निर्जित: सङ्ख्ये जरासन्धादयस्तथा ॥ २ ॥
verses.transliteration: śiśupāla-sakhaḥ śālvorukmiṇy-udvāha āgataḥyadubhir nirjitaḥ saṅkhyejarāsandhādayas tathā
verses.meaning: Śālva was a friend of Śiśupāla’s. When he attended the wedding of Rukmiṇī, the Yadu warriors defeated him in battle, along with Jarāsandha and the other kings.
verses.wordMeanings: śiśupāla; sakhaḥ; śālvaḥ; rukmiṇī; udvāhe; āgataḥ; yadubhiḥ; nirjitaḥ; saṅkhye; jarāsandha; ādayaḥ; tathā

verses.text: शाल्व: प्रतिज्ञामकरोच्छृण्वतां सर्वभूभुजाम् ।अयादवां क्ष्मां करिष्ये पौरुषं मम पश्यत ॥ ३ ॥
verses.transliteration: śālvaḥ pratijñām akarocchṛṇvatāṁ sarva-bhūbhujāmayādavāṁ kṣmāṁ kariṣyepauruṣaṁ mama paśyata
verses.meaning: Śālva swore in the presence of all the kings: “I will rid the earth of Yādavas. Just see my prowess!”
verses.wordMeanings: śālvaḥ; pratijñām; akarot; śṛṇvatām; sarva; bhū; bhujām; ayādavām; kṣmām; kariṣye; pauruṣam; mama; paśyata

verses.text: इति मूढ: प्रतिज्ञाय देवं पशुपतिं प्रभुम् ।आराधयामास नृप: पांशुमुष्टिं सकृद्ग्रसन् ॥ ४ ॥
verses.transliteration: iti mūḍhaḥ pratijñāyadevaṁ paśu-patiṁ prabhumārādhayām āsa nṛpaḥpāṁśu-muṣṭiṁ sakṛd grasan
verses.meaning: Having thus made his vow, the foolish King proceeded to worship Lord Paśupati [Śiva] as his deity by eating a handful of dust each day, and nothing more.
verses.wordMeanings: iti; mūḍhaḥ; pratijñāya; devam; paśu; patim; prabhum; āradhayām; āsa; nṛpaḥ; pāṁśu; muṣṭim; sakṛt; grasan

verses.text: संवत्सरान्ते भगवानाशुतोष उमापति: ।वरेणच्छन्दयामास शाल्वं शरणमागतम् ॥ ५ ॥
verses.transliteration: saṁvatsarānte bhagavānāśu-toṣa umā-patiḥvareṇa cchandayām āsaśālvaṁ śaraṇam āgatam
verses.meaning: The great Lord Umāpati is known as “he who is quickly pleased,” yet only at the end of a year did he gratify Śālva, who had approached him for shelter, by offering him a choice of benedictions.
verses.wordMeanings: saṁvatsara; ante; bhagavān; āśu; toṣaḥ; umā; patiḥ; vareṇa; chandayām; āsa; śālvam; śaraṇam; āgatam
verses.purport: Śālva worshiped Lord Śiva, who is famous as Āśutoṣa, “one who is quickly satisfied.” And yet Lord Śiva did not come to Śālva for an entire year because, being bhagavān, a great, all-knowing personality, he understood that any benediction given to Lord Kṛṣṇa’s enemy would be fruitless. Still, as stated by the words śaraṇam āgatam, Śālva had come to Lord Śiva for shelter, and thus to maintain the standard principle that a worshiper receives a benediction, Lord Śiva offered one to Śālva.

verses.text: देवासुरमनुष्याणां गन्धर्वोरगरक्षसाम् ।अभेद्यं कामगं वव्रे स यानं वृष्णिभीषणम् ॥ ६ ॥
verses.transliteration: devāsura-manuṣyāṇāṁgandharvoraga-rakṣasāmabhedyaṁ kāma-gaṁ vavresa yānaṁ vṛṣṇi-bhīṣaṇam
verses.meaning: Śālva chose a vehicle that could be destroyed by neither demigods, demons, humans, Gandharvas, Uragas nor Rākṣasas, that could travel anywhere he wished to go, and that would terrify the Vṛṣṇis.
verses.wordMeanings: deva; asura; manuṣyāṇām; gandharva; uraga; rakṣasām; abhedyam; kāma; gam; vavre; saḥ; yānam; vṛṣṇi; bhīṣaṇam

verses.text: तथेति गिरिशादिष्टो मय: परपुरंजय: ।पुरं निर्माय शाल्वाय प्रादात्सौभमयस्मयम् ॥ ७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tatheti giriśādiṣṭomayaḥ para-puraṁ-jayaḥpuraṁ nirmāya śālvāyaprādāt saubham ayas-mayam
verses.meaning: Lord Śiva said, “So be it.” On his order, Maya Dānava, who conquers his enemies’ cities, constructed a flying iron city named Saubha and presented it to Śālva.
verses.wordMeanings: tathā; iti; giri; śa; ādiṣṭaḥ; mayaḥ; para; puram; jayaḥ; puram; nirmāya; śālvāya; prādāt; saubham; ayaḥ; mayam

verses.text: स लब्ध्वा कामगं यानं तमोधाम दुरासदम् ।ययौ द्वारवतीं शाल्वो वैरं वृष्णिकृतं स्मरन् ॥ ८ ॥
verses.transliteration: sa labdhvā kāma-gaṁ yānaṁtamo-dhāma durāsadamyayas dvāravatīṁ śālvovairaṁ vṛṣṇi-kṛtaṁ smaran
verses.meaning: This unassailable vehicle was filled with darkness and could go anywhere. Upon obtaining it, Śālva went to Dvārakā, remembering the Vṛṣṇis’ enmity toward him.
verses.wordMeanings: saḥ; labdhvā; kāma; gam; yānam; tamaḥ; dhāma; durāsadam; yayau; dvāravatīm; śālvaḥ; vairam; vṛṣṇi; kṛtam; smaran

verses.text: निरुध्य सेनया शाल्वो महत्या भरतर्षभ ।पुरीं बभञ्जोपवनानुद्यानानि च सर्वश: ॥ ९ ॥सगोपुराणि द्वाराणि प्रासादाट्टालतोलिका: ।विहारान् स विमानाग्र्‍यान्निपेतु: शस्‍त्रवृष्टय: ॥ १० ॥शिला द्रुमाश्चाशनय: सर्पा आसारशर्करा: ।प्रचण्डश्चक्रवातोऽभूद् रजसाच्छादिता दिश: ॥ ११ ॥
verses.transliteration: nirudhya senayā śālvomahatyā bharatarṣabhapurīṁ babhañjopavanānudyānāni ca sarvaśaḥ
verses.meaning: Śālva besieged the city with a large army, O best of the Bharatas, decimating the outlying parks and gardens, the mansions along with their observatories, towering gateways and surrounding walls, and also the public recreational areas. From his excellent airship he threw down a torrent of weapons, including stones, tree trunks, thunderbolts, snakes and hailstones. A fierce whirlwind arose and blanketed all directions with dust.
verses.wordMeanings: nirudhya; senayā; śālvaḥ; mahatyā; bharata; ṛṣabha; purīm; babhañja; upavanān; udyanāni; ca; sarvaśaḥ; sa; gopurāṇi; dvārāṇi; prāsāda; aṭṭāla; tolikāḥ; vihārān; saḥ; vimāna; agryāt; nipetuḥ; śastra; vṛṣṭayaḥ; śilā; drumāḥ; ca; aśanayaḥ; sarpāḥ; āsāra; śarkarāḥ; pracaṇḍaḥ; cakravātaḥ; abhūt; rajasā; ācchādditāḥ; diśaḥ

verses.text: इत्यर्द्यमाना सौभेन कृष्णस्य नगरी भृशम् ।नाभ्यपद्यत शं राजंस्‍त्रिपुरेण यथा मही ॥ १२ ॥
verses.transliteration: ity ardyamānā saubhenakṛṣṇasya nagarī bhṛśamnābhyapadyata śaṁ rājaṁstri-pureṇa yathā mahī
verses.meaning: Thus terribly tormented by the airship Saubha, Lord Kṛṣṇa’s city had no peace, O King, just like the earth when it was attacked by the three aerial cities of the demons.
verses.wordMeanings: iti; ardyamānā; saubhena; kṛṣṇasya; nagarī; bhṛśam; na; abhyapadyata; śam; rājan; tri; pureṇa; yathā; mahī

verses.text: प्रद्युम्नो भगवान् वीक्ष्य बाध्यमाना निजा: प्रजा: ।मा भैष्टेत्यभ्यधाद् वीरो रथारूढो महायशा: ॥ १३ ॥
verses.transliteration: pradyumno bhagavān vīkṣyabādhyamānā nijāḥ prajāḥma bhaiṣṭety abhyadhād vīrorathārūḍho mahā-yaśāḥ
verses.meaning: Seeing His subjects so harassed, the glorious and heroic Lord Pradyumna told them, “Do not fear,” and mounted His chariot.
verses.wordMeanings: pradyumnaḥ; bhagavān; vīkṣya; bādhyamānāḥ; nijāḥ; prajāḥ; mā; bhaiṣṭa; iti; abhyadhāt; vīraḥ; ratha; ārūḍhaḥ; mahā; yaśāḥ

verses.text: सात्यकिश्चारुदेष्णश्च साम्बोऽक्रूर: सहानुज: ।हार्दिक्यो भानुविन्दश्च गदश्च शुकसारणौ ॥ १४ ॥अपरे च महेष्वासा रथयूथपयूथपा: ।निर्ययुर्दंशिता गुप्ता रथेभाश्वपदातिभि: ॥ १५ ॥
verses.transliteration: sātyakiś cārudeṣṇaś casāmbo ’krūraḥ sahānujaḥhārdikyo bhānuvindaś cagadaś ca śuka-sāraṇau
verses.meaning: The chief commanders of the chariot warriors — Sātyaki, Cārudeṣṇa, Sāmba, Akrūra and his younger brothers, along with Hārdikya, Bhānuvinda, Gada, Śuka and Sāraṇa — went out of the city with many other eminent bowmen, all girded in armor and protected by contingents of soldiers riding on chariots, elephants and horses, and also by companies of infantry.
verses.wordMeanings: sātyakiḥ; cārudeṣṇaḥ; ca; sāmbaḥ; akrūraḥ; saha; anujaḥ; hārdikyaḥ; bhānuvindaḥ; ca; gadaḥ; ca; śuka; sāraṇau; apare; ca; mahā; iṣv; āsāḥ; ratha; yūtha; pa; yūtha; pāḥ; niryayuḥ; daṁśitāḥ; guptāḥ; ratha; ibha; aśva; padātibhiḥ

verses.text: तत: प्रववृते युद्धं शाल्वानां यदुभि: सह ।यथासुराणां विबुधैस्तुमुलं लोमहर्षणम् ॥ १६ ॥
verses.transliteration: tataḥ pravavṛte yuddhaṁśālvānāṁ yadubhiḥ sahayathāsurāṇāṁ vibudhaistumulaṁ loma-harṣaṇam
verses.meaning: A tumultuous, hair-raising battle then commenced between Śālva’s forces and the Yadus. It equaled the great battles between the demons and demigods.
verses.wordMeanings: tataḥ; pravavṛte; yuddham; śālvānām; yadubhiḥ; saha; yathā; asurāṇām; vibudhaiḥ; tumulam; loma; harṣaṇam

verses.text: ताश्च सौभपतेर्माया दिव्यास्‍त्रै रुक्‍मिणीसुत: ।क्षणेन नाशयामास नैशं तम इवोष्णगु: ॥ १७ ॥
verses.transliteration: tāś ca saubha-pater māyādivyāstrai rukmiṇī-sutaḥkṣaṇena nāśayām āsanaiśaṁ tama ivoṣṇa-guḥ
verses.meaning: With His divine weapons Pradyumna instantly destroyed all of Śālva’s magic illusions, in the same way that the warm rays of the sun dissipate the darkness of night.
verses.wordMeanings: tāḥ; ca; saubha; pateḥ; māyāḥ; divya; astraiḥ; rukmiṇī; sutaḥ; kṣaṇena; nāśayām; āsa; naiśam; tamaḥ; iva; uṣṇa; guḥ

verses.text: विव्याध पञ्चविंशत्या स्वर्णपुङ्खैरयोमुखै: ।शाल्वस्य ध्वजिनीपालं शरै: सन्नतपर्वभि: ॥ १८ ॥शतेनाताडयच्छाल्वमेकैकेनास्य सैनिकान् ।दशभिर्दशभिर्नेतृन् वाहनानि त्रिभिस्‍त्रिभि: ॥ १९ ॥
verses.transliteration: vivyādha pañca-viṁśatyāsvarṇa-puṅkhair ayo-mukhaiḥśālvasya dhvajinī-pālaṁśaraiḥ sannata-parvabhiḥ
verses.meaning: Lord Pradyumna’s arrows all had gold shafts, iron heads and perfectly smooth joints. With twenty-five of them He struck down Śālva’s commander-in-chief [Dyumān], and with one hundred He struck Śālva himself. Then He pierced Śālva’s officers with one arrow each, his chariot drivers with ten arrows each, and his horses and other carriers with three arrows each.
verses.wordMeanings: vivyādha; pañca; viṁśatyā; svarṇa; puṅkhaiḥ; ayaḥ; mukhaiḥ; śālvasya; dhvajinī; pālam; śaraiḥ; sannata; parvabhiḥ; śatena; atāḍayat; śālvam; eka; ekena; asya; sainikān; daśabhiḥ; daśabhiḥ; netṝn; vāhanāni; tribhiḥ; tribhiḥ

verses.text: तदद्भुचतं महत् कर्म प्रद्युम्नस्य महात्मन: ।द‍ृष्ट्वा तं पूजयामासु: सर्वे स्वपरसैनिका: ॥ २० ॥
verses.transliteration: tad adbhutaṁ mahat karmapradyumnasya mahātmanaḥdṛṣṭvā taṁ pūjayām āsuḥsarve sva-para-sainikāḥ
verses.meaning: When they saw the glorious Pradyumna perform that amazing and mighty feat, all the soldiers on both sides praised Him.
verses.wordMeanings: tat; adbhutam; mahat; karma; pradyumnasya; mahā; ātmanaḥ; dṛṣṭvā; tam; pūjayām; āsuḥ; sarve; sva; para; sainikāḥ

verses.text: बहुरूपैकरूपं तद् द‍ृश्यते न च द‍ृश्यते ।मायामयं मयकृतं दुर्विभाव्यं परैरभूत् ॥ २१ ॥
verses.transliteration: bahu-rūpaika-rūpaṁ taddṛśyate na ca dṛśyatemāyā-mayaṁ maya-kṛtaṁdurvibhāvyaṁ parair abhūt
verses.meaning: At one moment the magic airship built by Maya Dānava appeared in many identical forms, and the next moment it was again only one. Sometimes it was visible, and sometimes not. Thus Śālva’s opponents could never be sure where it was.
verses.wordMeanings: bahu; rūpa; eka; rūpam; tat; dṛśyate; na; ca; dṛśyate; māyā; mayam; maya; kṛtam; durvibhāvyam; paraiḥ; abhūt

verses.text: क्व‍‍चिद्भ‍ूमौ क्व‍‍चिद् व्योम्नि गिरिमूर्ध्‍नि जले क्व‍‍चित् ।अलातचक्रवद् भ्राम्यत् सौभं तद् दुरवस्थितम् ॥ २२ ॥
verses.transliteration: kvacid bhūmau kvacid vyomnigiri-mūrdhni jale kvacitalāta-cakra-vad bhrāmyatsaubhaṁ tad duravasthitam
verses.meaning: From one moment to the next the Saubha airship appeared on the earth, in the sky, on a mountain peak or in the water. Like a whirling, flaming baton, it never remained in any one place.
verses.wordMeanings: kvacit; bhūmau; kvacit; vyomni; giri; mūrdhni; jale; kvacit; alāta; cakra; vat; bhrāmyat; saubham; tat; duravasthitam

verses.text: यत्र यत्रोपलक्ष्येत ससौभ: सहसैनिक: ।शाल्वस्ततस्ततोऽमुञ्चञ् छरान् सात्वतयूथपा: ॥ २३ ॥
verses.transliteration: yatra yatropalakṣyetasa-saubhaḥ saha-sainikaḥśālvas tatas tato ’muñcañcharān sātvata-yūthapāḥ
verses.meaning: Wherever Śālva would appear with his Saubha ship and his army, there the Yadu commanders would shoot their arrows.
verses.wordMeanings: yatra; yatra; upalakṣyeta; sa; saubhaḥ; saha; sainikaḥ; śālvaḥ; tataḥ; tataḥ; amuñcan; śarān; sātvata; yūtha; pāḥ

verses.text: शरैरग्‍न्यर्कसंस्पर्शैराशीविषदुरासदै: ।पीड्यमानपुरानीक: शाल्वोऽमुह्यत्परेरितै: ॥ २४ ॥
verses.transliteration: śarair agny-arka-saṁsparśairāśī-viṣa-durāsadaiḥpīḍyamāna-purānīkaḥśālvo ’muhyat pareritaiḥ
verses.meaning: Śālva became bewildered upon seeing his army and aerial city thus harassed by his enemy’s arrows, which struck like fire and the sun and were as intolerable as snake venom.
verses.wordMeanings: śaraiḥ; agni; arka; saṁsparśaiḥ; āśī; viṣa; durāsadaiḥ; pīḍyamāna; pura; anīkaḥ; śālvaḥ; amuhyat; para; īritaiḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Śrīdhara Svāmī explains that the arrows of the Yadu commanders burned like fire, struck simultaneously from all sides like the sun’s rays, and, like snake venom, were lethal by a single touch.

verses.text: शाल्वानीकपशस्‍त्रौघैर्वृष्णिवीरा भृशार्दिता: ।न तत्यजू रणं स्वं स्वं लोकद्वयजिगीषव: ॥ २५ ॥
verses.transliteration: śālvānīkapa-śastraughairvṛṣṇi-vīrā bhṛśārditāḥna tatyajū raṇaṁ svaṁ svaṁloka-dvaya-jigīṣavaḥ
verses.meaning: Because the heroes of the Vṛṣṇi clan were eager for victory in this world and the next, they did not abandon their assigned posts on the battlefield, even though the downpour of weapons hurled by Śālva’s commanders tormented them.
verses.wordMeanings: śālva; anīka; pa; śastra; oghaiḥ; vṛṣṇi; vīrāḥ; bhṛśa; arditāḥ; na; tatyajuḥ; raṇam; svam; svam; loka; dvaya; jigīṣavaḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Prabhupāda writes: “The heroes of the Yadu dynasty were determined to either die on the battlefield or gain victory. They were confident of the fact that if they would die in the fighting they would attain a heavenly planet, and if they would come out victorious they would enjoy the world.”

verses.text: शाल्वामात्यो द्युमान्नाम प्रद्युम्नं प्राक्प्रपीडित: ।आसाद्य गदया मौर्व्या व्याहत्य व्यनदद् बली ॥ २६ ॥
verses.transliteration: śālvāmātyo dyumān nāmapradyumnaṁ prak prapīḍitaḥāsādya gadayā maurvyāvyāhatya vyanadad balī
verses.meaning: Śālva’s minister Dyumān, previously wounded by Śrī Pradyumna, now ran up to Him and, roaring loudly, struck Him with his club of black steel.
verses.wordMeanings: śālva; amātyaḥ; dyumān; nāma; pradyumnam; prāk; prapīḍitaḥ; āsādya; gadayā; maurvyā; vyāhatya; vyanadat; balī

verses.text: प्रद्युम्नं गदया शीर्णवक्ष:स्थलमरिंदमम् ।अपोवाह रणात्सूतो धर्मविद् दारुकात्मज: ॥ २७ ॥
verses.transliteration: pradyumnaṁ gadayā sīrṇa-vakṣaḥ-sthalam ariṁ-damamapovāha raṇāt sūtodharma-vid dārukātmajaḥ
verses.meaning: Pradyumna’s driver, the son of Dāruka, thought that his valiant master’s chest had been shattered by the club. Knowing well his religious duty, he removed Pradyumna from the battlefield.
verses.wordMeanings: pradyumnam; gadayā; śīrṇa; vakṣaḥ; sthalam; arim; damam; apovāha; raṇāt; sūtaḥ; dharma; vit; dāruka; ātmajaḥ
verses.purport: Śrīla Viśvanātha Cakravartī explains that actually Lord Pradyumna has a sac-cid-ānanda body, an eternal, spiritual form that can never be wounded by mundane weapons. Dāruka’s son, however, was a great devotee of the Lord, and out of intense love he feared for the safety of his master and thus removed Him from the battlefield. Śrīla Prabhupāda writes: “The name of Śālva’s commander-in-chief was Dyumān. He was very powerful, and although bitten by twenty-five of Pradyumna’s arrows, he suddenly attacked Pradyumna with his fierce club and struck Him so strongly that Pradyumna became unconscious. Immediately there was a roaring, ‘Now He is dead! Now He is dead!’ The force of the club on Pradyumna’s chest was very severe, enough to tear asunder the chest of an ordinary man.”

verses.text: लब्धसंज्ञो मुहूर्तेन कार्ष्णि: सारथिमब्रवीत् ।अहो असाध्विदं सूत यद् रणान्मेऽपसर्पणम् ॥ २८ ॥
verses.transliteration: labdha-samjño muhūrtenakārṣṇiḥ sārathim abravītaho asādhv idaṁ sūtayad raṇān me ’pasarpaṇam
verses.meaning: Quickly regaining consciousness, Lord Kṛṣṇa’s son Pradyumna said to His charioteer, “O driver, this is abominable — for Me to have been removed from the battlefield!
verses.wordMeanings: labdha; saṁjñaḥ; muhūrtena; kārṣṇiḥ; sārathim; abravīt; aho; asādhu; idam; sūta; yat; raṇāt; me; apasarpaṇam

verses.text: न यदूनां कुले जात: श्रूयते रणविच्युत: ।विना मत्क्लीबचित्तेन सूतेन प्राप्तकिल्बिषात् ॥ २९ ॥
verses.transliteration: na yadūnāṁ kule jātaḥśrūyate raṇa-vicyutaḥvinā mat klība-cittenasūtena prāpta-kilbiṣāt
verses.meaning: “Except for Me, no one born in the Yadu dynasty has ever been known to abandon the battlefield. My reputation has now been stained by a driver who thinks like a eunuch.
verses.wordMeanings: na; yadūnām; kule; jātaḥ; śrūyate; raṇa; vicyutaḥ; vinā; mat; klība; cittena; sūtena; prāpta; kilbiṣāt

verses.text: किं नु वक्ष्येऽभिसङ्गम्य पितरौ रामकेशवौ ।युद्धात्सम्यगपक्रान्त: पृष्टस्तत्रात्मन: क्षमम् ॥ ३० ॥
verses.transliteration: kiṁ nu vakṣye ’bhisaṅgamyapitarau rāma-keśavauyuddhāt samyag apakrāntaḥpṛṣṭas tatrātmanaḥ kṣamam
verses.meaning: “What will I say to My fathers, Rāma and Keśava, when I return to Them after having simply fled the battle? What can I tell Them that will befit My honor?
verses.wordMeanings: kim; nu; vakṣye; abhisaṅgamya; pitarau; rāma; keśavau; yuddhāt; samyak; apakrāntaḥ; pṛṣṭaḥ; tatra; ātmanaḥ; kṣamam
verses.purport: Śrī Pradyumna here uses the word pitarau, “fathers,” loosely. Lord Balarāma was of course His uncle.

verses.text: व्यक्तं मे कथयिष्यन्ति हसन्त्यो भ्रातृजामय: ।क्लैब्यं कथं कथं वीर तवान्यै: कथ्यतां मृधे ॥ ३१ ॥
verses.transliteration: vyaktaṁ me kathayiṣyantihasantyo bhrātṛ-jāmayaḥklaibyaṁ kathaṁ kathaṁ vīratavānyaiḥ kathyatāṁ mṛdhe
verses.meaning: “Certainly My sisters-in-law will laugh at Me and say, ‘O hero, tell us how in the world Your enemies turned You into such a coward in battle.’”
verses.wordMeanings: vyaktam; me; kathayiṣyanti; hasantyaḥ; bhrātṛ; jāmayaḥ; klaibyam; katham; katham; vīra; tava; anyaiḥ; kathyatām; mṛdhe

verses.text: सारथिरुवाचधर्मं विजानतायुष्मन् कृतमेतन्मया विभो ।सूत: कृच्छ्रगतं रक्षेद् रथिनं सारथिं रथी ॥ ३२ ॥
verses.transliteration: sārathir uvācadharmaṁ vijānatāyuṣmankṛtam etan mayā vibhosūtaḥ kṛcchra-gataṁ rakṣedrathinaṁ sārathiṁ rathī
verses.meaning: The driver replied: O long-lived one, I have done this knowing full well my prescribed duty. O my Lord, the chariot driver must protect the master of the chariot when he is in danger, and the master must also protect his driver.
verses.wordMeanings: sārathiḥ; uvāca; dharmam; vijānatā; āyuḥ; man; kṛtam; etat; mayā; vibho; sūtaḥ; kṛcchra; gatam; rakṣet; rathinam; sārathim; rathī

verses.text: एतद्विदित्वा तु भवान्मयापोवाहितो रणात् ।उपसृष्ट: परेणेति मूर्च्छितो गदया हत: ॥ ३३ ॥
verses.transliteration: etad viditvā tu bhavānmayāpovāhito raṇātupasṛṣṭaḥ pareṇetimūrcchito gadayā hataḥ
verses.meaning: With this rule in mind, I removed You from the battlefield, since You had been struck unconscious by Your enemy’s club and I thought You were seriously injured.
verses.wordMeanings: etat; viditvā; tu; bhavān; mayā; apovāhitaḥ; raṇāt; upasṛṣṭaḥ; pareṇa; iti; mūrcchitaḥ; gadayā; hataḥ
